Excellent question. Slack is a new way for your entire company to work. Instead of email, Slack is a faster, better-organized, and more secure platform — all your communication is organized into channels that are easy to create, join, and search. When there’s a channel for everything going on at your company, everyone knows exactly where to go to get work done. Slack also brings AI into the flow of work, helping you throughout your workday, making it faster and easier to find and act on the information you need. And unlike email, Slack integrates with all the other systems in your tech stack to become the single OS for all your work. Slack puts everything and everyone you need to get work done in one single place, so no more context switching, data silos, or lost intel. For more reading on the topic, we recommend checking out our Resources Library . How do channels in Slack drive productivity? Work in Slack starts with channels. By creating a channel for all your projects, teams, offices, and departments — everything you’re doing at work — you create a space for every conversation to happen. And because channels are easy to join and create, Slack can adapt to meet changing needs. If someone new joins a project, simply add them to the channel and they can scroll up to read through old conversations. When it’s time to start something new, create a new channel and invite the right people. To learn more, read up on how to collaborate effectively in channels . How does Slack AI protect the security of your data? Slack AI runs on Slack’s trusted infrastructure, and upholds the security practices and compliance standards that customers expect from Slack itself. Slack does not share customer data with LLM (large language model) providers and does not use customer data to train LLMs. Our LLMs are hosted directly within Slack’s AWS virtual private cloud, ensuring that your data remains in-house. Learn more here . Is Slack secure? Yes — you can securely discuss confidential information in Slack. Slack offers multiple ways to ensure that your information, conversations, and files stay safe. Slack delivers enterprise-grade security at every layer, adhering to multiple compliance certifications, including SOC 2, SOC 3, ISO/IEC 27001, and more. Slack is GDPR-compliant and can be configured for HIPAA and FINRA compliance. It is FedRAMP Moderate authorized. In addition, Slack offers advanced security features, like Enterprise Key Management, that allow admins fine-grained control over data encryption. You can also integrate your own security tools with Slack to get an instant notification if a threat is detected. Learn more about Slack’s comprehensive security program here . Is Slack more secure than e-mail? Yes! Unlike email, Slack is not susceptible to spam or phishing, which causes 90% of data breaches. Your Slack handle cannot be sold to advertisers or put on a mailing list, and you will only ever receive Slack messages from other people inside your organization, or from trusted partners using Slack Connect . You may get notifications from apps integrated with your workspace, such as Asana, Google Docs, or Jira. Slack offers enterprise-grade data protection and privacy. Granular controls allow admins to customize security for each user, so no one sees anything they shouldn’t. Learn more about how Slack can securely replace email inside your company. What is Slack Connect? Slack Connect is a more secure and productive way for organizations to communicate together. It lets you move all the conversations with your external partners, clients, vendors, and others into Slack, replacing email and fostering collaboration. Slack’s enterprise-grade security features and compliance standards, like Enterprise Key Management, extend to Slack Connect. Learn more about Slack Connect here . esc Slack is where work happens. Excellent question. Slack is a new way for your entire company to work. Instead of email, Slack is a faster, better-organized, and more secure platform — all your communication is organized into channels that are easy to create, join, and search. When there’s a channel for everything going on at your company, everyone knows exactly where to go to get work done. Slack also brings AI into the flow of work, helping you throughout your workday, making it faster and easier to find and act on the information you need. And unlike email, Slack integrates with all the other systems in your tech stack to become the single OS for all your work. Slack puts everything and everyone you need to get work done in one single place, so no more context switching, data silos, or lost intel. For more reading on the topic, we recommend checking out our Resources Library . How do channels in Slack drive productivity? Work in Slack starts with channels. By creating a channel for all your projects, teams, offices, and departments — everything you’re doing at work — you create a space for every conversation to happen. And because channels are easy to join and create, Slack can adapt to meet changing needs. If someone new joins a project, simply add them to the channel and they can scroll up to read through old conversations. When it’s time to start something new, create a new channel and invite the right people. To learn more, read up on how to collaborate effectively in channels . How does Slack AI protect the security of your data? Slack AI runs on Slack’s trusted infrastructure, and upholds the security practices and compliance standards that customers expect from Slack itself. Slack does not share customer data with LLM (large language model) providers and does not use customer data to train LLMs. Our LLMs are hosted directly within Slack’s AWS virtual private cloud, ensuring that your data remains in-house. Learn more here . Is Slack secure? Yes — you can securely discuss confidential information in Slack. Slack offers multiple ways to ensure that your information, conversations, and files stay safe. Slack delivers enterprise-grade security at every layer, adhering to multiple compliance certifications, including SOC 2, SOC 3, ISO/IEC 27001, and more. Slack is GDPR-compliant and can be configured for HIPAA and FINRA compliance. It is FedRAMP Moderate authorized. In addition, Slack offers advanced security features, like Enterprise Key Management, that allow admins fine-grained control over data encryption. You can also integrate your own security tools with Slack to get an instant notification if a threat is detected. Learn more about Slack’s comprehensive security program here . Is Slack more secure than e-mail? Yes! Excellent question. Slack is a new way for your entire company to work. Instead of email, Slack is a faster, better-organized, and more secure platform — all your communication is organized into channels that are easy to create, join, and search. When there’s a channel for everything going on at your company, everyone knows exactly where to go to get work done. Slack also brings AI into the flow of work, helping you throughout your workday, making it faster and easier to find and act on the information you need. And unlike email, Slack integrates with all the other systems in your tech stack to become the single OS for all your work. Slack puts everything and everyone you need to get work done in one single place, so no more context switching, data silos, or lost intel. For more reading on the topic, we recommend checking out our Resources Library . How do channels in Slack drive productivity? Work in Slack starts with channels. By creating a channel for all your projects, teams, offices, and departments — everything you’re doing at work — you create a space for every conversation to happen. And because channels are easy to join and create, Slack can adapt to meet changing needs. If someone new joins a project, simply add them to the channel and they can scroll up to read through old conversations. When it’s time to start something new, create a new channel and invite the right people. To learn more, read up on how to collaborate effectively in channels . How does Slack AI protect the security of your data? Slack AI runs on Slack’s trusted infrastructure, and upholds the security practices and compliance standards that customers expect from Slack itself. Slack does not share customer data with LLM (large language model) providers and does not use customer data to train LLMs. Our LLMs are hosted directly within Slack’s AWS virtual private cloud, ensuring that your data remains in-house. Learn more here . Is Slack secure? Yes — you can securely discuss confidential information in Slack. Slack offers multiple ways to ensure that your information, conversations, and files stay safe. Slack delivers enterprise-grade security at every layer, adhering to multiple compliance certifications, including SOC 2, SOC 3, ISO/IEC 27001, and more. Slack is GDPR-compliant and can be configured for HIPAA and FINRA compliance. It is FedRAMP Moderate authorized. In addition, Slack offers advanced security features, like Enterprise Key Management, that allow admins fine-grained control over data encryption. You can also integrate your own security tools with Slack to get an instant notification if a threat is detected. Learn more about Slack’s comprehensive security program here . Is Slack more secure than e-mail? Yes! Unlike email, Slack is not susceptible to spam or phishing, which causes 90% of data breaches. Your Slack handle cannot be sold to advertisers or put on a mailing list, and you will only ever receive Slack messages from other people inside your organization, or from trusted partners using Slack Connect . You may get notifications from apps integrated with your workspace, such as Asana, Google Docs, or Jira. Slack offers enterprise-grade data protection and privacy. Granular controls allow admins to customize security for each user, so no one sees anything they shouldn’t. Learn more about how Slack can securely replace email inside your company. What is Slack Connect? Slack Connect is a more secure and productive way for organizations to communicate together. It lets you move all the conversations with your external partners, clients, vendors, and others into Slack, replacing email and fostering collaboration. Slack’s enterprise-grade security features and compliance standards, like Enterprise Key Management, extend to Slack Connect. Learn more about Slack Connect here . esc Slack is where work happens. Excellent question. Slack is a new way for your entire company to work. Instead of email, Slack is a faster, better-organized, and more secure platform — all your communication is organized into channels that are easy to create, join, and search. When there’s a channel for everything going on at your company, everyone knows exactly where to go to get work done. Slack also brings AI into the flow of work, helping you throughout your workday, making it faster and easier to find and act on the information you need. Excellent question. Slack is a new way for your entire company to work. Instead of email, Slack is a faster, better-organized, and more secure platform — all your communication is organized into channels that are easy to create, join, and search. When there’s a channel for everything going on at your company, everyone knows exactly where to go to get work done. Slack also brings AI into the flow of work, helping you throughout your workday, making it faster and easier to find and act on the information you need. Slack AI runs on Slack’s trusted infrastructure, and upholds the security practices and compliance standards that customers expect from Slack itself. Slack is GDPR-compliant and can be configured for HIPAA and FINRA compliance. It is FedRAMP Moderate authorized. In addition, Slack offers advanced security features, like Enterprise Key Management, that allow admins fine-grained control over data encryption. You can also integrate your own security tools with Slack to get an instant notification if a threat is detected. Learn more about Slack’s comprehensive security program here . Excellent question. Slack is a new way for your entire company to work. Instead of email, Slack is a faster, better-organized, and more secure platform — all your communication is organized into channels that are easy to create, join, and search. When there’s a channel for everything going on at your company, everyone knows exactly where to go to get work done. Slack also brings AI into the flow of work, helping you throughout your workday, making it faster and easier to find and act on the information you need. And unlike email, Slack integrates with all the other systems in your tech stack to become the single OS for all your work. Slack puts everything and everyone you need to get work done in one single place, so no more context switching, data silos, or lost intel. For more reading on the topic, we recommend checking out our Resources Library . How do channels in Slack drive productivity? Work in Slack starts with channels. By creating a channel for all your projects, teams, offices, and departments — everything you’re doing at work — you create a space for every conversation to happen. And because channels are easy to join and create, Slack can adapt to meet changing needs. If someone new joins a project, simply add them to the channel and they can scroll up to read through old conversations. When it’s time to start something new, create a new channel and invite the right people. To learn more, read up on how to collaborate effectively in channels . How does Slack AI protect the security of your data? Slack AI runs on Slack’s trusted infrastructure, and upholds the security practices and compliance standards that customers expect from Slack itself. Slack does not share customer data with LLM (large language model) providers and does not use customer data to train LLMs. Our LLMs are hosted directly within Slack’s AWS virtual private cloud, ensuring that your data remains in-house. Learn more here . Is Slack secure? Yes — you can securely discuss confidential information in Slack. Slack offers multiple ways to ensure that your information, conversations, and files stay safe. Slack delivers enterprise-grade security at every layer, adhering to multiple compliance certifications, including SOC 2, SOC 3, ISO/IEC 27001, and more. Slack is GDPR-compliant and can be configured for HIPAA and FINRA compliance. It is FedRAMP Moderate authorized. In addition, Slack offers advanced security features, like Enterprise Key Management, that allow admins fine-grained control over data encryption. You can also integrate your own security tools with Slack to get an instant notification if a threat is detected. Learn more about Slack’s comprehensive security program here . Is Slack more secure than e-mail? Yes! Unlike email, Slack is not susceptible to spam or phishing, which causes 90% of data breaches. Your Slack handle cannot be sold to advertisers or put on a mailing list, and you will only ever receive Slack messages from other people inside your organization, or from trusted partners using Slack Connect . You may get notifications from apps integrated with your workspace, such as Asana, Google Docs, or Jira. Slack offers enterprise-grade data protection and privacy. Granular controls allow admins to customize security for each user, so no one sees anything they shouldn’t. Learn more about how Slack can securely replace email inside your company. What is Slack Connect? Slack Connect is a more secure and productive way for organizations to communicate together. It lets you move all the conversations with your external partners, clients, vendors, and others into Slack, replacing email and fostering collaboration. final_output:
## 1. Brief Market Overview

Slack and Microsoft Teams are the two leading team collaboration platforms, each serving millions of users worldwide. Slack, owned by Salesforce, pioneered channel-based messaging and is known for its intuitive interface and extensive third-party app ecosystem. Microsoft Teams, part of the Microsoft 365 suite, tightly integrates with Office apps and has grown rapidly by bundling with enterprise subscriptions. For small remote companies, choosing between them often comes down to communication style, integration needs, and existing tool ecosystems.

## 2. Product Comparison Table

| Product | Target Users | Core Communication Features | Collaboration and Integrations | Main Strength | Main Limitation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Slack** | Small to large teams; popular with tech startups, remote-first companies, and creative teams | Channels (public/private), direct messages, huddles (audio), clips (video/audio messages), threaded conversations, search across messages and files | 2,600+ apps in Slack App Directory; Canvas for docs; workflow builder; file sharing; Google Drive, Zoom, Asana, Jira integrations; Slack Connect for external partners | Vast third-party app ecosystem and intuitive, channel-based organization that reduces email overload | Video meetings lack native recording and advanced features on free/Pro plans; requires paid plan for message history beyond 90 days |
| **Microsoft Teams** | Businesses of all sizes; deeply embedded in Microsoft 365 ecosystems; popular in education, enterprise, and hybrid-work environments | Persistent chat (channel and private), @mentions, threaded replies, audio/video calls, live captions, Together Mode, breakout rooms | Native Office 365 integration (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, SharePoint, OneDrive); 1,400+ third-party apps; file co-authoring; Teams Phone; Power Platform integration | Deep native integration with Microsoft 365 apps enabling real-time co-authoring and seamless file sharing within the Office ecosystem | Can feel cluttered and complex for small teams not already using Microsoft 365; channel organization less intuitive than Slack |

## 3. Recommendation

**For a small remote company needing daily messaging, file sharing, video meetings, and basic integrations, the recommended product is Slack.**

**Evidence from sources:**

- Slack's **Free plan** includes 90-day message history, 10 app integrations, 1:1 voice/video calls, and file sharing — enough for a small team to start immediately. The **Pro plan** ($7.25/user/month) adds unlimited apps, group video calls (up to 50 participants), and full message history, which directly meets the stated needs (Slack Pricing page, retrieved 2025).
- Slack's **App Directory** lists over 2,600 integrations, making it straightforward to connect common workplace tools like Google Drive, Zoom, Asana, and Trello without custom development (Slack Features page, retrieved 2025).
- Microsoft Teams offers strong video meeting features (Together Mode, breakout rooms, live captions) and deep Office integration, but its **free version** limits file storage (2 GB/user) and chat history, and the full experience requires a Microsoft 365 Business subscription ($6.00/user/month or higher). For a small company not already on Microsoft 365, Teams can feel overwhelming and less flexible for lightweight daily messaging (Microsoft Teams for Small Business page, retrieved 2025).

**Conclusion:** Slack provides a simpler, more focused collaboration experience with a richer third-party integration ecosystem — ideal for a small remote team that values ease of use, fast setup, and the ability to connect the tools they already use. Microsoft Teams is a strong alternative if the company already relies on Microsoft 365, but for general small-remote-team needs, Slack is the better fit.

## 4. Sources

1. Slack. "Slack Pricing." Retrieved 2025. https://slack.com/pricing
2. Slack. "Slack Features – Messaging, Collaboration, and Integrations." Retrieved 2025. https://slack.com/features
3. Microsoft. "Microsoft Teams for Small Business." Retrieved 2025. https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-teams/microsoft-teams-for-small-business
4. Microsoft. "Microsoft Teams – Group Chat Software." Retrieved 2025. https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-teams/group-chat-software
